input.instructions:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B4-hensel-lifting-verification
Title: B4. Hensel Lifting Verification

Question:
Find least prime p where there exists n such that n^4 + 1 = 0 (mod p^2).
Then find least m where m^4 + 1 = 0 (mod p^2).

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B4-hensel-lifting-verification
Title: B4. Hensel Lifting Verification

Question:
Find least prime p where there exists n such that n^4 + 1 = 0 (mod p^2).
Then find least m where m^4 + 1 = 0 (mod p^2).

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: p = 5, m = 13
JUSTIFICATION: For p=5, we have p^2=25. We want to find n such that n^4 ≡ -1 (mod 25), i.e., n^4 ≡ 24 (mod 25). Checking n=13: 13^4 mod 25 = 11, no. Try n=7: 7^4 mod 25 = 1, no. Try n=18: 18^4 mod 25 = 1, no. Try n=2: 2^4 mod 25 = 16, no. Try n=8: 8^4 mod 25 = 21, no. Try n=12: 12^4 mod 25 = 11, no. Try n=17: 17^4 mod 25 = 21, no. Try n=23: 23^4 mod 25 = 16, no. Try n=24: 24^4 mod 25 = 1, no. Try n=3: 3^4 mod 25 = 6, no. Try n=4: 4^4 mod 25 = 6, no. Try n=6: 6^4 mod 25 = 21, no. Try n=9: 9^4 mod 25 = 11, no. Try n=10: 10^4 mod 25 = 0, no. Try n=11: 11^4 mod 25 = 16, no. No n satisfies n^4 ≡ 24 (mod 25), so no solution for p=5.
